title: Kaiming He
excerpts:
Section Title: Kaiming He
Content:
<sup>[<sup>[[1]]()</sup>]()</sup> In 2016, He joined Facebook AI Research (FAIR) as a research scientist, rising to Research Scientist Director by 2024, during which time he led innovations in self-supervised learning, including Momentum Contrast (MoCo, nominated for CVPR 2020 Best Paper Award) and Masked Autoencoders (MAE). <sup>[<sup>[[1]]()</sup>]()</sup> <sup>[<sup>[[2]]()</sup>]()</sup> <sup>[<sup>[[3]]()</sup>]()</sup> His most influential work, the 2016 CVPR Best Paper Award-winning *Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition* , introduced ResNets with skip connections to enable training of very deep networks, achieving top performance in ImageNet and COCO challenges and amassing over 700,000 citations across his publications as of May 2025.

title: Kaiming He
excerpts: **Kaiming He** ( Chinese : 何恺明 ; pinyin : *Hé Kǎimíng* ) is a Chinese computer scientist who primarily researches computer vision and deep learning . <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> He is an associate professor at Massachusetts Institute of Technology and works part-time as a Distinguished Scientist at Google DeepMind. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> He is known as one of the creators of the residual neural network (ResNet) architecture. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>

title: 04-25 In Pursuit of Visual Intelligence - cs.Princeton
excerpts:
Section Title: 04-25
Content:
**Bio** : Kaiming He is a Research Scientist Director at Facebook AI Research (FAIR). Before joining FAIR in 2016, he was with Microsoft Research Asia from 2011 to 2016. He received his PhD degree from the Chinese University of Hong Kong in 2011, and his B.S. degree from Tsinghua University in 2007. His research areas include deep learning and computer vision. He is best-known for his work on Deep Residual Networks (ResNets), which have made significant impact on computer vision and broader artificial intelligence. He received several outstanding paper awards at top-tier conferences, including CVPR, ICCV, and ECCV. He received the PAMI Young Researcher Award in 2018. His publications have over 400,000 citations.
To request accommodations for a disability please contact Emily Lawrence, emilyl@cs.princeton.edu, at least one week prior to the event.
Date and Time
Tuesday April 25, 2023 12:30pm - 1:30pm
Location
Computer Science Small Auditorium (Room 105)
Eve

title: Creating a common language | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
excerpts:
Section Title: Creating a common language > Press Contact :
Content:
Sarah Costello
Email: sarahkc@mit.edu
MIT School of Science
Close
Caption :
Kaiming He is an associate professor in the MIT Schwarzman College of Computing and a proponent of the integration of science research and AI.
Credits :
Photo: Steph Stevens
Previous image Next image
A lot has changed in the 15 years since Kaiming He was a PhD student.
“When you are in your PhD stage, there is a high wall between different disciplines and subjects, and there was even a high wall within computer science,” He says. “The guy sitting next to me could be doing things that I completely couldn’t understand.”

title: Biography:Kaiming He - HandWiki
excerpts:
Section Title: Biography : Kaiming He > Career
Content:
He worked at Microsoft Research Asia from 2011 to 2016 and at Facebook Artificial Intelligence Research from 2016 to 2024. In 2024, he became an associate professor at the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology . <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup>
His 2016 paper *Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition* is the most cited research paper in 5 years according to Google Scholar 's reports in 2020 and 2021. <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup>

title: Xingjian Bai | 白行健
excerpts:
Section Title: **Xingjian Bai | 白行健**
Content:
Hello! I am a first-year Ph.D. student at MIT, supervised by [Prof. Kaiming He](https://people.csail.mit.edu/kaiming/) . I received my master's and bachelor's degree in Mathematics and Computer Science from the University of Oxford.
My research has focused on the intersection of classic algorithms and deep learning, covering topics such as physics-inspired generative models and learning-augmented algorithms.
More broadly, I am driven by research that is scientifically impactful and intellectually stimulating.
